facts.title: A mountainous country
facts.fact: About 70% of South Korea is mountainous, so cities cluster in valleys and along the coast. Hiking is one of the most popular weekend activities.
facts.korean_en: There are many mountains in Korea.
facts.note: 주말에 등산을 해요 = "I hike on the weekend." 등산 literally means "climb-mountain."

facts.title: Jeju, the volcanic island
facts.fact: Jeju Island off the south coast is volcanic and home to Hallasan, South Korea's highest peak at 1,947 m. It is a favorite honeymoon and holiday destination.
facts.korean_en: Jeju Island is a beautiful island.

facts.title: The Han River through Seoul
facts.fact: The Han River runs through the middle of Seoul. Its riverside parks are popular for picnics, bike rides, and ordering fried chicken by delivery.
facts.korean_en: I ride a bicycle at the Han River.
facts.note: **치맥** (치킨 + 맥주, "chicken + beer") by the river is a beloved tradition.

facts.title: Four distinct seasons
facts.fact: Korea has four clear seasons: spring with cherry blossoms, hot humid summer, crisp colorful autumn, and a cold dry winter with snow.
facts.note: **사계절** = "four seasons." 벚꽃 (cherry blossom) season in spring is a big event.
